articles.title: Diagnostic capability of artificial intelligence tools for detecting and classifying odontogenic cysts and tumors: a systematic review and meta-analysis
articles.authors: Tobias RSF; Teodoro AB; Evangelista K; Leite AF; Valladares-Neto J; Silva BSF; Yamamoto-Silva FP; Almeida FT; Garcia Silva MA
articles.journal: Oral Surgery, Oral Medicine, Oral Pathology and Oral Radiology
articles.keywords: Ameloblastoma; Odontogenic Cysts; Artificial intelligence; CBCT; Meta-analysis
articles.abstract: Objective: To evaluate the diagnostic capability of AI for detecting and classifying odontogenic cysts and tumors, with emphasis on odontogenic keratocyst (OKC) and ameloblastoma. Nine electronic databases were examined. Human-based studies using AI algorithms to detect or classify odontogenic cysts and tumors using panoramic radiographs or CBCT were included. A meta-analysis was performed for classifying OKCs and ameloblastomas. Results: Twelve studies concluded that AI is a promising tool for detection and/or classification of lesions, producing high diagnostic test values. Three articles assessed CNNs in classifying OKC and ameloblastoma: accuracy was 0.893 (95% CI 0.832–0.954). AI applied to CBCT produced superior accuracy. Conclusion: AI tools exhibited relatively high accuracy in detecting and classifying OKC and ameloblastoma. Panoramic radiography appears accurate for AI-based classification, albeit with low certainty.

articles.title: Improved Diagnostic Accuracy of Ameloblastoma and Odontogenic Keratocyst on Cone-Beam CT by Artificial Intelligence
articles.authors: Chai ZK; Mao L; Chen H; Sun TG; Shen XM; Liu J; Sun ZJ
articles.keywords: Inception v3; ameloblastoma; cone-beam CT; convolutional neural network; deep learning; odontogenic keratocyst
articles.abstract: Objective: To utilize a convolutional neural network (CNN) to make preoperative differential diagnoses between ameloblastoma (AME) and odontogenic keratocyst (OKC) on CBCT. Methods: CBCT images of 178 AMEs and 172 OKCs were retrieved from Wuhan University. Datasets were split into training (272 cases) and testing (78 cases). The Inception v3 algorithm was utilized and compared with oral and maxillofacial surgeons. Results: Sensitivity, specificity, accuracy, and F1 score were 87.2%, 82.1%, 84.6%, and 85.0%, respectively. Senior surgeons achieved 65.7% and junior surgeons 58.0% accuracy. Conclusion: The AI model outperformed junior surgeons and was comparable to senior surgeons, suggesting great clinical potential.

articles.title: Computer tomographic differential diagnosis of ameloblastoma and odontogenic keratocyst: classification using a convolutional neural network
articles.journal: Journal of Applied Clinical Medical Physics
articles.keywords: Ameloblastoma; Artificial intelligence; Odontogenic Cysts; Tomography; X-Ray computed
articles.abstract: Objective: To analyse automatic classification performance of Google Inception v3 using tomographic images of OKCs and ameloblastomas. Methods: Axial multidetector CT images from 22 ameloblastoma and 18 OKC patients were selected; 350 images were segmented manually and augmented to 2500. K-fold cross-validation was used. Results: Accuracy ranged from 90.16% to 92.48% across five iterations. A higher error rate was observed for ameloblastoma classification. Conclusion: High classification accuracy was demonstrated for tomographic images of OKCs and ameloblastomas.

articles.title: Differential diagnosis of ameloblastoma and odontogenic keratocyst by machine learning of panoramic radiographs
articles.authors: Zhang L; Zhao Y; Wang Z
articles.keywords: Ameloblastoma; Deep learning; Machine learning; Odontogenic keratocyst
articles.abstract: Purpose: To propose a CNN-based algorithm to improve classification of ameloblastoma and OKC from 420 panoramic radiographs. A dual-branch transfer learning CNN was proposed. Results: Accuracy 90.36% (AUC=0.946); sensitivity 92.88%; specificity 87.80%. Outperformed VGG-19 (80.72%) and ResNet-50 (78.31%). Conclusions: The proposed algorithm significantly improves differential diagnosis accuracy.

articles.title: Deep Learning for Automated Detection of Cyst and Tumors of the Jaw in Panoramic Radiographs
articles.authors: Yang H; Jo E; Kim HJ; Cha IH; Jung YS; Nam W; Kim JY; Kim JW; Kim SM; Lee JH; Huh KH
articles.keywords: YOLO; artificial intelligence; computer-assisted diagnosis; deep learning; odontogenic cysts; panoramic radiography
articles.abstract: 1602 lesions on panoramic radiographs from 2010–2019 (Yonsei University) were classified into dentigerous cysts, OKC, ameloblastoma, and no cyst. YOLO was compared to oral and maxillofacial surgeons. YOLO precision=0.707, recall=0.680. Differences between machine and clinicians were statistically insignificant. Results indicate usefulness of auto-detecting CNNs for pathology detection in oral and maxillofacial surgery.

articles.title: Automatic segmentation of ameloblastoma on CT images using deep learning with limited data
articles.authors: Xu L; Qiu K; Li K; Ying G; Huang X; Zhu X
articles.keywords: Ameloblastoma; Deep learning; Mask R-CNN; CT segmentation; Artificial intelligence
articles.abstract: Background: Ameloblastoma necessitates accurate localization and segmentation for effective diagnosis and treatment. Methods: CT images from 79 patients with ameloblastoma were used to train and test a Mask R-CNN model with image preprocessing and cross-validation. An external validation set of 200 CT images was also used. Results: DICE index=0.874; AP (IoU 0.5–0.95)=0.741; AP (IoU 0.5)=0.914; AP (IoU 0.75)=0.826. External validation confirmed strong generalization. Conclusion: The AI-based segmentation shows notable advantages in efficiency, accuracy, and speed for clinical diagnosis.

articles.title: Classification and prognostic evaluation of ameloblastoma using multiplanar CT imaging: a retrospective analysis
articles.authors: Xu L; Zhang J; Fang M; Li X
articles.keywords: Ameloblastoma; Computed tomography; Imaging classification; Locularity; Prognosis
articles.abstract: Background: Ameloblastoma has a significant recurrence rate. CT allows reassessment of traditional locularity classification. Methods: Panoramic radiographs and CT of 255 histopathologically confirmed ameloblastoma patients were analyzed. Results: On CT: 46.3% pseudo-multilocular, 29.4% unilocular, 24.3% multilocular. Cortical perforation, root resorption, and size correlated significantly with CT locularity. Recurrence rate=14.11%. Conclusion: 3D imaging modalities such as CT are essential for accurate ameloblastoma diagnosis and surgical planning.

articles.title: Face the Future—Artificial Intelligence in Oral and Maxillofacial Surgery
articles.authors: Miragall MF; Knoedler S; Kauke-Navarro M; et al.
articles.keywords: artificial intelligence; machine learning; oral and maxillofacial surgery; deep learning; diagnosis
articles.abstract: AI has brought unprecedented potential to oral and maxillofacial surgery (OMS). This review summarizes AI applications in OMS: diagnosis, treatment planning, surgical navigation, and outcome prediction. AI models show particular promise in automated detection and classification of odontogenic cysts and tumors, ameloblastomas, and OKCs via CBCT and panoramic radiographs. Challenges include data scarcity, algorithm interpretability, and regulatory considerations. Future directions include multimodal AI, federated learning, and deployment in resource-limited settings.

articles.title: Artificial Intelligence Models Accuracy for Odontogenic Keratocyst Detection From Panoramic View Radiographs: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis
articles.authors: Shoorgashti R; Jafari F; et al.
articles.keywords: OKC; artificial intelligence; YOLO; panoramic radiograph; meta-analysis; deep learning
articles.abstract: OKC is a radiolucent jaw lesion often mistaken for ameloblastomas on panoramic radiographs. This systematic review and meta-analysis evaluated the diagnostic accuracy of AI models in detecting OKC. 12 studies (2019–2024) from Korea, Japan, China, Croatia, and Austria were included. AI models, particularly YOLO-based architectures, can improve OKC diagnostic accuracy in panoramic radiographs. While AI shows strong capabilities in simple cases, it should complement, not replace, human expertise in complex situations.
